element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary interface for match customization; real player names and positions introduced as core feature
Multiple camera angles available; player learns to switch perspectives for tactical advantage
Realistic passing mechanics emphasized; core mechanic loop
Tackles, headers, bicycle kicks available; require skill timing
Transfer and team customization tools; long-form progression structure

typical_relief_triggers: Camera angle adjustment for clearer view
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Simulation-driven; pacing matches real soccer flow with variable intensity based on match situation and player positioning

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible team/league selection; tutorial-level match setup with customizable difficulty
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Opponent AI behavior increases in sophistication; requires understanding of realistic passing controls and positioning
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship-level league play; advanced tactical execution needed; competitive AI requires mastery of game mechanics
notable_spikes: Transition from practice matches to league competition
AI behavioral adaptation as league progresses
Animation/frame rate inconsistencies on lower-spec platforms creating skill ceiling variance

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard match structure with pre-match setup, live play, and post-match results. Supports single-player vs AI or multiplayer vs human opponent.
Extended progression through multiple matches within organized league structure; cumulative standing and team management between matches
Pre-match preparation allows team roster changes, player transfers, and tactical setup; creates personalization layer before competitive play

sources_used: FIFA Soccer 96 - Wikipedia (reception, gameplay features, platform information)
